items.title: Optional survey income field
items.scenario: A customer survey asks for annual income. Younger respondents leave it blank more often, but among respondents with the same age and education, missing income looks random. The team treats this as MAR, adds a missingness flag, and imputes income with median within education bands fit on the training split.
items.explanation: MAR missingness can be related to observed demographics without depending on the hidden income itself. Group-wise median imputation on train plus an indicator preserves both level and the fact that income was not reported.

items.title: Sensor dropout in IoT temperature logs
items.scenario: A factory sensor reports temperature every minute but drops packets for 30 seconds during Wi-Fi handoff. The pipeline sorts by timestamp and forward-fills short gaps, then flags runs longer than five minutes as device faults rather than imputing.
items.explanation: Ordered data often uses forward-fill for brief MCAR-like dropouts. Long gaps are a different failure mode—imputing them would invent a false steady signal.

items.title: Clinical lab with listwise deletion
items.scenario: A small clinical trial has 200 patients and only 3% missing on two lab features used in a linear risk score. Exploratory plots show no link between missingness and age or sex. The team uses complete-case analysis after confirming enough rows remain.
items.explanation: Low MCAR missingness on a few columns can justify listwise deletion when sample size stays adequate and bias risk is low.

items.title: Credit application with native NaN handling
items.scenario: A credit dataset has 40% missing on optional employer tenure because many applicants are self-employed. Mean imputation collapses the distinction between "not applicable" and "zero years." The team keeps NaN and trains LightGBM, which learns separate split behavior for missing employer tenure.
items.explanation: High, structurally informative missingness sometimes is better preserved than flattened to a constant. Tree ensembles with native NaN support are a common choice here.
